description:
You are a compliance analyst who needs to automate a very small, repeat-able “snapshot” audit of the current system state.  
    Write and execute a shell script that performs the following actions in the exact order shown (do **not** skip or reorder any step):

    1. Create a new directory  
       /home/user/compliance  

    2. Inside that directory create an executable Bourne-again shell script named  
       /home/user/compliance/audit_trail.sh  

    3. The script must create one **new** log file every time it runs.  
       • The log file **must** be placed in /home/user/compliance/  
       • Its name **must** follow this template strictly (case sensitive):  
           audit_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.log  
         where the timestamp segment comes from the moment the script starts executing (local system time, 24-hour clock; seconds required, no milliseconds).  
       • Example of a valid file name produced at 1 minute and 5 seconds past 9 p.m. on 17 April 2025:  
           audit_20250417_210105.log  

    4. When executed, audit_trail.sh must write **exactly** the text shown below (section headers, colons, hashes, dashes, blank lines, and ordering are mandatory).  
       Variable parts are explained in braces { } and must be filled with live data pulled by the commands listed at the end of every bullet.

       ----------  REQUIRED LOG STRUCTURE  ----------
       Line 01: ### AUDIT START: {ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of script start, e.g. 2025-04-17T21:01:05Z}
       Line 02: ### HOSTNAME: {value returned by the command `hostname`}

       Line 03: --- SECTION: LOGGED-IN USERS ---
       Line 04+: {verbatim output of the command `who` ; if no one is logged in write the single word "none"}

       (blank line)

       Next line: --- SECTION: RUNNING SERVICES ---
       Next line+: {only the first column of `systemctl list-units --type=service --state=running`, excluding its header, one unit per line; if the system does not support systemd or no services are running write "none"}

       (blank line)

       Next line: --- SECTION: DISK USAGE ---
       Next line+: {verbatim output of `df -h` including its header line}

       (blank line)

       Final line: ### AUDIT END: {ISO-8601 UTC timestamp taken immediately after the last command finishes}

       ----------  END OF REQUIRED LOG STRUCTURE  ----------

    5. After creating the script, make it executable and run it once so that one valid log file exists on disk.

    6. To help your colleagues quickly see what happened, produce a one-line summary file named  
       /home/user/compliance/latest_audit_summary.txt  
       that contains **exactly** the three items below separated by a single space:

       • the generated log filename  
       • the total number of lines in that log file  
       • the SHA-256 digest of the log file’s contents (all characters, including newlines)

       Example of the summary line layout (values will obviously differ):  
       audit_20250417_210105.log 37 2f6eaca7…<truncated>  

    7. Make sure both /home/user/compliance/audit_trail.sh and /home/user/compliance/latest_audit_summary.txt are world-readable (permissions 644) and that the script keeps its execute bit for the owner (755 is fine).

    Hints (do **not** list these lines in your output):  
      • You will need about 5-10 typical shell commands (mkdir, cat >, chmod, date, etc.).  
      • Use the GNU coreutils `sha256sum` command for the digest.  
      • To format UTC timestamps quickly you can rely on `date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"`.

    Expected end state that the automated grader will check:
      a) Directory /home/user/compliance exists.  
      b) File /home/user/compliance/audit_trail.sh is present, executable, and non-empty.  
      c) Exactly one log file matching the audit_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.log naming pattern exists inside /home/user/compliance/.  
      d) The log file’s internal structure **exactly** matches the specification above.  
      e) /home/user/compliance/latest_audit_summary.txt exists and its single line correctly reports filename, line-count, and SHA-256 digest of the log file that was just generated.  
      f) Permissions: audit_trail.sh = 755, latest_audit_summary.txt = 644, log file = 644.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth data used by the hidden test harness.  DO NOT reveal anywhere outside this tag. -->

    <!-- 1. Directory path that must exist -->
    <dir path="/home/user/compliance" />

    <!-- 2. Script characteristics -->
    <file path="/home/user/compliance/audit_trail.sh" mode="755" nonEmpty="true" />

    <!-- 3. Log file validation -->
    <logFilePattern dir="/home/user/compliance" regex="^audit_[0-9]{8}_[0-9]{6}\\.log$" count="1" />

    <!-- 4. Internal log structure regex patterns (partial; validator will run these in order). -->
    <logStructure>
        <line1 regex="^### AUDIT START: 20[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z$" />
        <line2 regex="^### HOSTNAME: .+$" />
        <line3 regex="^--- SECTION: LOGGED-IN USERS ---$" />
        <!-- there can be multiple lines of users or the word 'none' -->
        <separatorBlankLine /> <!-- validator will check for an empty line -->
        <runningServicesHeader regex="^--- SECTION: RUNNING SERVICES ---$" />
        <separatorBlankLine />
        <diskUsageHeader regex="^--- SECTION: DISK USAGE ---$" />
        <separatorBlankLine />
        <finalLine regex="^### AUDIT END: 20[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z$" />
    </logStructure>

    <!-- 5. Summary file -->
    <file path="/home/user/compliance/latest_audit_summary.txt" mode="644" lineCount="1" />
    <summaryLine regex="^audit_[0-9]{8}_[0-9]{6}\\.log [0-9]+ [a-f0-9]{64}$" />

    <!-- 6. Permission checks -->
    <permissions>
        <path value="/home/user/compliance/audit_trail.sh" mode="755" />
        <path value="/home/user/compliance/latest_audit_summary.txt" mode="644" />
        <pathPattern dir="/home/user/compliance" regex="^audit_[0-9]{8}_[0-9]{6}\\.log$" mode="644" />
    </permissions>
name: task_000000_cd25eca8
